messages.content: Build an Effect v3 wrapper from scratch that converts a native fetch() Promise into an Effect with a tagged NetworkError using Effect.tryPromise.
Build an Effect v3 program from scratch that converts bidirectionally between Effect and Promise: wrap a Promise with Effect.tryPromise, then convert back to Promise via Effect.runPromise.
What's the Effect v3 way to convert an fp-ts TaskEither into an Effect.Effect? Show a minimal example that preserves the typed error channel.
Build from scratch: a helper that converts fp-ts TaskEither<ApiError, User> to Effect.Effect<User, ApiError>, then compose it with an Effect.gen that fetches a profile.
Build an Effect v3 Stream from scratch that consumes an AsyncIterable<number> and sums the values via Stream.runForEach.
What's the Effect v3 way to read every chunk of an AsyncIterable<string> as a Stream and log each line? Minimal example.
Build an Effect v3 race from scratch using Effect.raceAll over three candidate data sources with a bounded timeout as the fourth racer.
What's the Effect v3 way to combine Effect.raceAll with a custom Effect.raceWith so the winner cancels the loser and a side effect runs on whichever resolves first? Minimal example.
Explain briefly the difference between Effect.orDie and Effect.die in Effect v3, then show a working typed implementation that promotes a recoverable error to a defect on purpose.
What's the Effect v3 way to serialise access to a shared counter with a mutex-like latch? Show a minimal example with two concurrent incrementers.
Write an Effect v3 program that races three HTTP fetches with a 500ms timeout. Constraints: strict types, tagged error class, no any, Effect.raceAll.
Write a v3 helper that promotes any unexpected error in a user-scoped handler to a defect. Constraints: use Effect.orDie for known handler errors, keep ValidationError recoverable, no any.
Write an Effect v3 program that runs two Promise-bound tasks. Constraints: one uses Effect.promise (defects on reject), one uses Effect.tryPromise (typed error), and the composition is Effect.all.
Write a v3 pipeline that reads an AsyncIterable<Buffer> from Node stdin, splits into lines, and prints each line. Constraints: Stream.fromAsyncIterable, Stream.runForEach, no any.
Write a v3 mutex wrapper. Constraints: one Effect.makeSemaphore(1), generic over any effect, preserves typed E and R, no any.
Explain briefly how Effect.promise differs from Effect.tryPromise, then show a working typed implementation that picks the right one for each of two cases.
What's the Effect v3 way to bridge an fp-ts Either into an Effect without any plumbing? Minimal example.
Explain briefly what a defect is in Effect v3, then show a working typed implementation that uses Effect.die to mark an invariant violation inside a pure computation.
What's the Effect v3 way to race a user task against a cancel signal represented as a Latch? Minimal example.
Explain briefly how Effect.raceAll picks a winner and what happens to losers in Effect v3, then show a working typed implementation with three candidates and a timeout.
Build from scratch: a v3 helper that takes a TaskEither<E, A> and retries it via Effect.retry with a composed Schedule.
Build from scratch: an Effect v3 rate-limited batch processor that takes an AsyncIterable<number> and sums squares, capped at concurrency 4.
What's the Effect v3 way to turn an Effect back into a Promise for interop with a non-Effect callsite? Minimal example.
Build from scratch: a v3 file-watcher-style pipeline that turns an AsyncIterable<string> of file paths into a Stream, dedupes consecutive duplicates, and logs each unique one.
Build from scratch: a v3 composed raceAll + raceWith demo where raceAll picks a winner from the cache cluster, and raceWith pairs that against a live-origin fetch.
Build an Effect v3 program from scratch that uses Effect.makeLatch as a one-shot signal: one fiber awaits the latch, another opens it after a delay.
What's the v3 way to run a TaskEither-producing library call inside an Effect.gen without losing error typing? Minimal example.
What's the Effect v3 way to spawn N workers that drain a queue but are capped by a semaphore? Minimal example.
